items.question:
Considering only Bong Joon Ho’s Oscars shown in the video and his two drinking jokes, which option correctly describes the chronological order of these six milestones?

(1) Winning Original Screenplay; (2) Winning International Feature; (3) First time he says he is “ready to drink tonight… until next morning”; (4) Winning Directing; (5) Second time he says he will “drink until next morning”; (6) Winning Best Picture.

options.A: Original Screenplay → International Feature → Directing → first drinking joke → Best Picture → second drinking joke.
options.B: International Feature → Original Screenplay → first drinking joke → Directing → Best Picture → second drinking joke.
options.C: Original Screenplay → International Feature → first drinking joke → Directing → second drinking joke → Best Picture.
options.D: Original Screenplay → Directing → first drinking joke → International Feature → second drinking joke → Best Picture.

items.answer_text: Original Screenplay → International Feature → first drinking joke → Directing → second drinking joke → Best Picture.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: On-screen text identified them as winners of the 'Original Screenplay' for 'Parasite.'
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: The category 'Original Screenplay' appears as text on the stage backdrop and lower-third; recognizing this win depends on reading the visual category label rather than the spoken content.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A large, illuminated 'INTERNATIONAL FEATURE FILM' sign glows on stage... She continues, noting, "This is the first movie from South Korea to be nominated and to win the Oscar." Bong Joon Ho... accepts the golden Oscar statuette...
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying this as the International Feature win requires both the visible category sign and the presenter’s spoken explanation that it is the first South Korean film to win.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: He adds, "I'm ready, ready to drink tonight," eliciting laughter and more applause before he concludes, "Until next morning. Thank you."
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must hear Bong Joon Ho’s words to know this is the first ‘drink tonight / until next morning’ joke, and visually see that it occurs during the International Feature speech.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A female announcer's voice declares, "This is the second Oscar tonight and the third nomination for Bong Joon Ho." ... a prominent 'DIRECTING' sign. Bong Joon Ho, now at the podium... begins his acceptance speech...
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining that this is the Directing win requires the spoken line about his second Oscar tonight plus the visible 'DIRECTING' category sign.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Bong Joon Ho... humorously stating, "Thank you. I will drink until next morning. Thank you," to loud applause and cheering.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must listen to his words to catch this second, separate ‘drink until next morning’ line and see that he is again on stage holding an Oscar.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The winners of 'Parasite' then take the stage... under a circular, starry backdrop prominently displaying 'BEST PICTURE'.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Recognizing this as the Best Picture win relies on both the spoken Best Picture announcement and the visible 'BEST PICTURE' sign behind the group.

items.question: Comparing Zarol’s earlier clash with the Colossal Shadowy Entity in the snowy mist to Kena’s final confrontation with the same creature at the ancient stone gate, which option best describes how Kena’s method of dealing with the entity differs from Zarol’s?

options.A: Zarol relies on a towering stone guardian to shield him while he quietly supports from behind, whereas Kena faces the entity alone, charging her purple staff to hurl a destructive pink blast straight into its chest.
options.B: Zarol drives the entity back by channeling his purple‑orb staff into a radiant pink blast that engulfs and harms it, whereas Kena, after being knocked down, sits in a meditative pose and releases calm blue energy that forms a sigil above her instead of directly attacking.
options.C: Zarol weakens the entity by entering a meditative trance that summons a glowing sigil in the sky, whereas Kena confronts it by firing repeated energy arrows and detonating them in rapid succession around its body.
options.D: Zarol attempts to cleanse the entity by placing relics on an altar and channeling blue healing light into it, whereas Kena rejects ritual methods and instead charges straight in with continuous melee strikes and close‑range staff explosions.

items.answer_text: Zarol drives the entity back by channeling his purple‑orb staff into a radiant pink blast that engulfs and harms it, whereas Kena, after being knocked down, sits in a meditative pose and releases calm blue energy that forms a sigil above her instead of directly attacking.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The Bearded Man rises, holding the staff aloft, and walks toward a colossal, shadowy figure emerging from the mist—a towering, antlered entity with a moss-covered body and glowing green eyes... The Bearded Man’s expression shifts from anguish to fierce resolve as he raises the staff, his voice growing more desperate, demanding, "Answer me!"
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Both the visuals (him advancing with the purple-orb staff toward the entity) and the audio (his anguished demands) establish the start and intent of Zarol’s confrontation.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: As the Bearded Man regains his footing, he raises his staff, channeling the purple orb’s energy with a powerful whooshing sound into a radiant pink blast that engulfs the entity. The entity recoils with a pained shriek and distorted roar, its form flickering and distorting under the magical assault...
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying his method as an aggressive, destructive blast requires the visual of the pink energy engulfing the entity and the audio of the magical whoosh and the creature’s pained roars.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The Young Girl is thrown to the ground, her staff clattering beside her, as the entity looms above. She quickly regains composure, closing her eyes and entering a meditative state, sitting cross-legged in the snow. As she focuses... radiant blue energy emanates from her, forming a glowing sigil in the sky above.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must see her knocked down and then meditating while blue energy and a sigil appear, and hear the shift in music to recognize this as a calm, ritual-like release rather than a direct attack.

items.question: Based on the full match timeline, which option correctly describes, in chronological order, (1) which team first takes the lead in games, (2) which team wins the first set, and (3) which team ultimately wins the mixed doubles match?

options.A: Williams/Tiafoe lead early in games, Bencic/Federer win the first set, and Bencic/Federer also win the overall match.
options.B: Bencic/Federer lead early in games, Bencic/Federer win the first set, but Williams/Tiafoe ultimately win the overall match.
options.C: Williams/Tiafoe lead early in games, Williams/Tiafoe win the first set, but Bencic/Federer ultimately win the overall match.
options.D: Bencic/Federer lead early in games, Williams/Tiafoe win the first set, but Bencic/Federer come back to win the overall match.

items.answer_text: Williams/Tiafoe lead early in games, Bencic/Federer win the first set, and Bencic/Federer also win the overall match.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Williams and Tiafoe eventually took the first game, with commentators praising Tiafoe's disguised serve. They then led 1-0, with the game score at 15-15.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must see the scoreboard showing 1-0 and also rely on the commentator’s verbal confirmation that the opening game went to the Americans.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A commentator notes, "it's 2-1 to America," adding that "Switzerland has one challenge remaining." The scoreboard confirms Williams/Tiafoe leading 2-1 against Bencic/Federer.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining that the Americans still lead later in the set requires both the spoken score announcement and the visual scoreboard.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A commentator announces, "Game and set," noting that Federer and Bencic have wrapped up the opening set… The camera then cuts to a graphic displaying the Set 1 Summary, showing Bencic/Federer winning 4-2.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying who wins the first set depends on the commentator’s call and the Set 1 Summary graphic.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The match culminates with an announcer declaring "Match point for the Swiss to get the victory over the Americans," and "Game, set, and match, Switzerland!" as Bencic/Federer secure the win.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Knowing who ultimately wins the match requires the audio call plus seeing it is Switzerland’s pair on the winning side.

items.question: Between the moment the fire is first set up under the shelter and the moment the metal samovar is placed over that fire, which activity takes place in the intervening interval?

options.A: They cook beef and vegetables together in a large black bowl over the fire, seasoning the dish before eventually bringing out the metal samovar.
options.B: They stay by the fire, immediately position the metal samovar over the flames, and then begin chopping more logs beside the wooden stump.
options.C: They remain under the shelter, continue chopping additional wood on the stump, and rearrange the basket of vegetables and terracotta vase on the wooden table.
options.D: They walk to the wooded hillside, collect water at the root cellar using a terracotta jug, and pour it into the metal bucket with a central spout before returning toward the shelter.

items.answer_text: They walk to the wooded hillside, collect water at the root cellar using a terracotta jug, and pour it into the metal bucket with a central spout before returning toward the shelter.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The match ignites with a distinct whoosh, and a small flame appears, producing rising smoke and the immediate crackling of the fire. The "person with bucket and stick" then adds larger pieces of wood, adjusting the structure to encourage the steadily growing flame, while the "person in brown vest" remarks, "So, here, now already."
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying the fire being set up under the shelter relies on seeing the kindling structure and flames (visual) and hearing the match ignition and crackling fire plus the spoken remark (audio).

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: As the fire intensifies, the person with bucket and stick steps back, and another individual in a brown quilted vest joins them. They walk away from the fire area, which continues to *crackle loudly*, toward a wooded hillside covered in fallen leaves, carrying a bucket and a stick, moving toward a small, dark opening.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: We must see that they leave the fire area and head toward the hillside/root cellar (visual) while the loud crackling fire and movement sounds indicate the fire is left burning (audio).

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The person in the brown vest crouches at the entrance, reaching into a dark, mossy opening to fill a terracotta jug with water from a natural spring, accompanied by the distinct sounds of water gurgling and flowing. After the jug is filled, the person in the brown vest hands it over, and the other individual pours the water into the metal bucket, creating continuous pouring and filtering sounds and ripples around the central spout.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining that they collect and pour water at the root cellar requires seeing the jug, root cellar, and metal bucket actions (visual) and using the gurgling, pouring, and filtering sounds to confirm the water activity (audio).

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The two individuals then walk away from the root cellar across a rocky, leaf-strewn field toward a covered shelter in the distance, with the dog following closely behind.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: This step is about their return journey from the root cellar toward the shelter; it is established purely by observing their movement and the landscape, without needing audio cues.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Under the shelter, the person with bucket and stick tends to a small fire on a metal tray, its steady crackling sounds audible as flames grow. They place a large metal samovar over the flames, which soon begins to bubble and boil, and adjust it with a stick.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying the moment when the samovar is placed over the fire requires seeing the samovar being set on the tray (visual) and hearing the crackling fire and subsequent bubbling/boiling (audio).

items.question: Considering the full timeline, how does the quiet, nearly empty Special Express ride to Shinagawa relate in order to the TeamLab Planets visit that ends with the vlogger outside Shinagawa Station mentioning plans to go to Akihabara?

options.A: The quiet Special Express ride to Shinagawa occurs between two parts of the TeamLab Planets visit, with the vlogger briefly leaving for Shinagawa and then returning to finish TeamLab before heading to Akihabara.
options.B: The quiet Special Express ride to Shinagawa happens earlier in the day, after the PASMO reload and before the TeamLab Planets visit that later ends with the vlogger again outside Shinagawa Station planning to go to Akihabara.
options.C: The quiet Special Express ride to Shinagawa happens later in the day, after the TeamLab Planets visit and the exterior Shinagawa scene where the vlogger mentions going to Akihabara.
options.D: The quiet Special Express ride and the Shinagawa scene about going to Akihabara are the same single visit to Shinagawa, shown from inside the station first and then outside.

items.answer_text: The quiet Special Express ride to Shinagawa happens earlier in the day, after the PASMO reload and before the TeamLab Planets visit that later ends with the vlogger again outside Shinagawa Station planning to go to Akihabara.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: At a station, a distinct "ding" sound is heard before the vlogger reloads their PASMO card with 2,000 yen at a white ticket machine, guided by Japanese voice instructions, as they insert the card and cash, then retrieve the updated card and a printing receipt.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying this as the morning setup for the later trip requires both seeing the PASMO reload on the machine screen and hearing the station/machine announcements.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The scene then transitions to a subway platform with yellow tactile paving, where a train arrives amidst train bell chimes and a Japanese female announcement stating a "Special Express for Haneda Airport is arriving" and instructing passengers to "Please stand away from the doors." The train doors open with a distinct sound, revealing a clean, nearly empty car with red and blue patterned seats. The vlogger boards and sits, expressing appreciation for the quietness with a heart emoji. As the train moves, an English male announcement identifies "The next stop is Shinagawa, Nobiki, Kamata," followed by a Japanese male announcement confirming arrival at Shinagawa.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining that this is the quiet Special Express ride to Shinagawa depends on both the spoken announcements and the visual of the nearly empty car and Shinagawa destination text.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Upon arrival, the vlogger exits into a bustling Shinagawa Station concourse, filled with commuters, their muffled chatter and footsteps contributing to the energetic atmosphere. The vlogger navigates past ticket gates and directional signs, including a green 'Transfer to JR Line' sign and a yellow 'Not Exit of Keikyu Line' warning.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: This shows the first arrival at Shinagawa immediately after the quiet Special Express; both signage visuals and ambient station sounds are needed to anchor it.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The journey ends at TeamLab Planets, a modern white building with bold signage, where the vlogger joins a queue under a white tent, noting they booked the activity via Klook weeks ago.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Linking urban travel to the TeamLab visit relies on seeing the TeamLab building and tent plus hearing the vlogger talk about heading there and the Klook booking.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The atmosphere shifts from the surreal, dark, and immersive digital environment to a bright, natural, and then urban setting, as the final scenes show the exterior of Shinagawa Station, with city sounds, people walking and waiting at a bus stop. On-screen text indicates the vlogger’s plan to go to Akihabara after TeamLab Planets, with the vlogger concluding, "thats all 😂 its a great and a must experience when going here to Tokyo," capturing the transition from immersive art to city exploration.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: To know this Shinagawa appearance happens after TeamLab and leads toward Akihabara, one must see the exterior station view and text and hear the closing narration.

items.question:
Focusing on one complete cycle that begins when the Samurai is defeated by beasts, then shows him forcing open a barred wooden door underwater, progressing through stone tunnels with skeletal remains, and finally reaching the point where he is fighting masked warriors, which of the following option lists shows the correct chronological order of the *first* underwater-door scene in that cycle and the *last* tunnel/courtyard fight that concludes it?

(Each option lists: the correct underwater-door scene that starts the chain → the intermediate tunnel segment → the final battle location where that chain ends.)

options.A: Underwater room where he picks the door lock and emerges into a castle courtyard duel → later tunnel segment with an unseen attacker at a door → masked warriors ambushing him in a tunnel battle.
options.B: Underwater room where he picks the door lock and emerges into a castle courtyard duel → tunnel and courtyard sequence that ends with him resurfacing into a stone courtyard over a fallen masked warrior → masked warriors ambushing him in a tunnel battle.
options.C: Underwater barred door after defeat by beasts leading into a tunnel → later tunnel segment with an unseen attacker at a door → masked warriors ambushing him in a tunnel battle.
options.D: Underwater barred door after defeat by beasts leading into a tunnel → tunnel and courtyard sequence that ends with him resurfacing into a stone courtyard over a fallen masked warrior → masked warriors ambushing him in a tunnel battle.

items.answer_text: Underwater barred door after defeat by beasts leading into a tunnel → later tunnel segment with an unseen attacker at a door → masked warriors ambushing him in a tunnel battle.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A powerful "thud" marks his defeat before the scene transitions to a monochromatic, underwater environment, filled with debris and rising bubbles, where muffled gurgling sounds indicate his struggle. The Samurai, gasping with strained grunts, battles to open a heavy, barred wooden door, his face contorted in pain as bubbles escape his mouth.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Both the visuals (underwater, barred door, bubbles) and the audio (gurgling, strained grunts, thud) are required to identify this as the specific cycle that begins with defeat by beasts and leads into a submerged barred wooden door.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: He then emerges from the water with a loud, defiant scream or roar and significant water splashing, standing defiantly, his Katana drawn. The camera cuts to a dimly lit, tense, and claustrophobic stone tunnel, where the Samurai's heavy, labored breathing and deliberate footsteps echo through the rough stone walls. His flickering torch casts deep shadows, intermittently revealing scattered skeletal remains on the ground.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Recognizing that this underwater-door sequence leads directly into a stone tunnel with skeletal remains and torchlight relies on both the visual transition (emerging from water into a tunnel) and the accompanying audio (scream, breathing, footsteps, torch crackle).

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The Samurai cautiously moves forward into a dark stone tunnel, his torch illuminating rough stone walls and scattered skeletal remains. He encounters a heavy wooden door, but is suddenly attacked from behind by an unseen assailant, marked by a loud metallic clang and a heavy thud. Thrown to the ground, his katana clatters beside him, and he struggles to rise, blood visible on his hand and chest.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Matching this as a later tunnel segment in the same overall tunnel progression requires the visuals of the tunnel, door, and skeletal remains plus the audio of the attack (clang, thud, katana clatter).

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A tense, percussive score begins, punctuated by the rhythmic, heavy footsteps and armor clanking of a group of masked warriors marching down the tunnel towards him. With a determined grunt, the Samurai slowly rises, his scarred and tattooed face grim, and draws his katana with a sharp *shing* sound. The combat erupts into a brutal, fast-paced sequence, filled with the loud *clang* of sword clashes… Despite his ferocity… he is eventually overwhelmed and thrown to the ground with a heavy impact. The masked warriors close in, their armor clanking.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Identifying this as the culmination of the tunnel cycle where masked warriors finally arrive in the tunnel requires seeing the masked warriors and tunnel setting and also hearing their approaching footsteps and armor clanking.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: He approaches a heavy, barred wooden door, its creaking and groaning audible as he attempts to open it, but is suddenly attacked from behind with a sharp impact and more grunts of struggle. The scene shifts to a dark, submerged environment where his gurgling and muffled struggles are heard amidst rising bubbles, before he resurfaces with a splash and gasping breaths into a stone courtyard. He stands over the fallen masked warrior, then turns and walks toward the door… In the background, the heavy, rhythmic footsteps and clanking armor of approaching Masked Warriors are heard.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: This connects a barred wooden door in a tunnel to a submerged sequence that leads into a stone courtyard and approaching masked warriors; both the visual transitions (door → underwater → courtyard) and audio cues (door creak, gurgling, footsteps, armor) are needed.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The sequence opens in a dimly lit, submerged room where the Samurai, partially underwater, struggles to open a heavy wooden door… With a final 'clunk,' the door opens, and he resurfaces, gasping loudly for air… He then moves through a dark, narrow stone tunnel… Emerging from the tunnel, the Samurai steps into a vast, open courtyard of a traditional Japanese-style castle… Another armed figure in similar attire appears in the distance, and the two face off in the center of the courtyard.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Distinguishing this underwater-door sequence as the one that leads specifically to a castle courtyard duel (not the tunnel masked-warrior battle) requires the visual environments (submerged room, tunnel, castle courtyard, second samurai) and the audio transitions (door clunk, gasps, music).

items.question: Based on the ordering of the segments shown, how does the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt with Fallon and The Roots relate in time to the extended card‑trick sequence?

options.A: Blaine starts with the card tricks, then does the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt a single time only after all the card material is finished.
options.B: Blaine does the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt only once, before any of the card tricks begin, and never returns to it afterward.
options.C: Blaine performs the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt only after the card tricks, and the early material shows cards but no frog at all.
options.D: Blaine does the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt both before and after the long block of card tricks, effectively bookending the card segment.

items.answer_text: Blaine does the frog/“Human Aquarium” stunt both before and after the long block of card tricks, effectively bookending the card segment.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Jimmy Fallon, the Man in gray suit with striped tie, inspects it closely, tilting it with curiosity... Fallon then announces, "David Blaine, everybody!" and confirms, "That's a real... that's a real frog!" ... Blaine performs the trick again, resulting in another round of cheers, gasps, and applause, followed by Blaine saying, "Thank you guys." The scene transitions to a graphic overlay with swirling smoke and golden text reading 'BEST OF DAVID BLAINE'...
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must see Blaine produce the frog and the bottle inspection plus hear Fallon confirm it as a real frog and introduce Blaine before the BEST OF graphic to know this frog/Human Aquarium stunt occurs in this early segment.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: David Blaine, a man with short dark hair and a beard wearing a black blazer, performs a series of card tricks for Jimmy Fallon... Blaine begins by stating, "simple, simple, simple. We just do simple," as he performs a card trick... In another trick, Blaine asks Fallon to name any card, and Fallon says, "Nine of Diamonds." ... The group then counts the scattered cards, confirming there are 20, to which Blaine exclaims, "Oh my god!"
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining this long middle segment is exclusively cards and continuous requires watching the visual handling of cards and hearing the card instructions, counts, and named cards.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: David Blaine... speaks and gestures before taking a long drink from a fluted clear glass bottle... As Blaine finishes drinking, he dramatically tilts his head back, causing a loud splash of liquid to erupt... Jimmy Fallon then takes the bottle, examines it closely... as David Blaine reveals, "That's a real frog." The audience applauds and cheers, and Jimmy Fallon exclaims, "David Blaine, everybody!" Blaine then begins a card trick, shuffling a deck... The Central figure then asks Jimmy Fallon about his special... before the Central figure introduces a concept he worked on, "the Human Aquarium." He explains, "100 years ago, there's a guy that converted his stomach into an aquarium," where he could "store live creatures" and "bring them up at will"...
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must see the second frog production and bottle inspection and hear Fallon call it a real frog and Blaine introduce the Human Aquarium concept to know the frog stunt recurs after the card run.

items.question: Across the full timeline from pre‑race expectations through the race outcome to the final celebrations, how does Tennessee’s result compare to the other two main contenders (Florida and Texas), and which of these teams is still shown celebrating a victorious result at the very end of the video?

options.A: Texas, Tennessee, and Florida are all named as top contenders; Tennessee appears to win the race ahead of Florida and Texas and keeps that win after review; in the closing shots both Tennessee and Florida are shown celebrating their victorious finishes while Texas is not.
options.B: Texas, Tennessee, and Florida are all named as top contenders; Florida appears to win the race ahead of Tennessee and Texas but is later disqualified so Texas takes first and Tennessee moves to second; in the closing shots Tennessee is still shown celebrating a successful result while Florida is not.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: Two announcers provide enthusiastic commentary, identifying Texas, Tennessee, and Florida as potential top three contenders, while noting Indiana's potential to break the American record despite being fourth, and Arizona State's anticipated strength in the later legs.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: You must hear which teams are named as the main contenders and see the lane layout; both the spoken identification and the on-screen team lanes are needed.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: As the camera follows the swimmers executing the butterfly stroke, NC State takes an early lead. The on-screen timer tracks the race progress, displaying times like 9.89, 13.5, and 20.24 seconds, while the leaderboard updates to show NC State in the lead.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Determining that NC State, not one of the three pre‑race contenders, is leading early requires both seeing the leaderboard and hearing the commentary calling out the split and leader.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: The on-screen timer and leaderboard dynamically update, showing Florida as the leader. As the race concludes, the Race Announcer, visible poolside in a white shirt, excitedly declares the final time of "05" (1:20.05), proclaiming it the "fastest in history" and that "all the records smashed," though noting it's "under review." The leaderboard confirms Florida in first, Tennessee in second, and Texas in third.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: To know Florida appears to win with Tennessee second and Texas third, you must see the leaderboard ordering and hear the announcer describe Florida’s apparent record win and the fact it is under review.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: A Color Commentator, a distinct male voice, expresses surprise with an emphatic "Oh! Florida's DQ'd!" as the disqualifications are announced. He elaborates, "Three disqualifications and the third one erases Florida's win and record setting time," and later exclaims, "Oh my gosh," lamenting the outcome and the "40 points that go to Texas." The scene then cuts to an underwater view, where the On-Screen Timer and Leaderboard confirms the final results: Texas wins with a new NCAA Record of 1:20.28, followed by Tennessee and California.
trajectory_with_timestamps.label: audio-visual
trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Understanding the reversal of results (Florida DQ, Texas winning, Tennessee moving to second) requires hearing the DQ announcement and seeing the updated results on the leaderboard.

items.question:

trajectory_with_timestamps.reason: Dialogue is needed to know Cyborg is now trapped in the Night Begins to Shine dimension and that only the song’s magic can reach him, while visuals show the team back in the living room without Cyborg.

trajectory_with_timestamps.evidence: In a dim, icy cavern, Cyborg, seated in a damaged mechanical wheelchair, excitedly presents a glowing pink cassette tape labeled 'B.E.R.' to his teammates...
